title: triticum dicoccum subsp. dicoccoides
intro: Triticum dicoccum subsp. dicoccoides, commonly known as wild einkorn, is an ancient subspecies of the einkorn wheat plant, characterized by its hard, hulled grains and slender spikes. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Triticum dicoccum subsp. dicoccoides include its ability to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and improve blood sugar control. Wild einkorn can be prepared as an herbal supplement in the form of capsules, teas, or flour, and is also used as a food ingredient in gluten-free baked goods and other recipes.
